groups.instruction: Answer the questions based on the audio.
groups.prompt_html:
MULTIPLE CHOICE 77

Questions 21 - 26

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

21 According to Anna, why is Humboldt now largely forgotten?

A Many of his theories have been disproved.
B His ideas just seem like common sense these days.
C Some of the behaviour he advocated is damaging to the environment.

22 How does Martin summarise Humboldt's thinking about the natural world?

A It is driven by animals' quest for food.
B It is a system in which things affect each other.
C It is always changing in ways that are difficult to predict.

23 What do Martin and Anna agree was the main reason that Humboldt was so famous in his lifetime?

A He was often seen with other famous people.
B He became an expert in many different fields.
C His research was relevant to ordinary people.

24 Anna says that Humboldt's books were popular because

A they were an appealing mixture of science and literature.
B they explained complex ideas in an accessible way.
C they contained practical advice that people could use.

25 Martin and Anna compare Humboldt's books to Wikipedia to make the point that

A they were regularly updated.
B they contained an enormous amount of detail.
C many different contributors helped to write them.

26 What is Martin doing when he mentions President Jefferson?

A explaining how Humboldt formed his views on slavery
B demonstrating how influential Humboldt was in America
C criticising Humboldt for not challenging Jefferson's behaviour

items.prompt_html: According to Anna, why is Humboldt now largely forgotten?

What do Martin and Anna agree was the main reason that Humboldt was so famous in his lifetime?
Anna says that Humboldt's books were popular because
Martin and Anna compare Humboldt's books to Wikipedia to make the point that
What is Martin doing when he mentions President Jefferson?

options.text: explaining how Humboldt formed his views on slavery

options.label: B

options.label: C
options.text: criticising Humboldt for not challenging Jefferson's behaviour
